scope_note: QA Fibonacci Depth Equidistribution cert [422]. Primary sources: Hecke, E. (1920) 'Eine neue Art von Zetafunktionen und ihre Beziehungen zur Verteilung der Primzahlen' Mathematische Zeitschrift 6 pp. 11-51 (Hecke L-functions for Q(sqrt 5); equidistribution of Frobenius in ray class groups implies equidistribution of delta(p)); Chebotarev, N. (1926) 'Die Bestimmung der Dichtigkeit einer Menge von Primzahlen welche zu einer gegebenen Substitutionsklasse gehoeren' Mathematische Annalen 95 pp. 191-228 (Chebotarev density theorem; Frobenius equidistribution over Gal(Q(sqrt 5)/Q)); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition alpha(p); original Fibonacci structure mod p); Pearson, K. (1900) 'On the criterion that a given system of deviations from the probable in the case of a correlated system of variables is such that it can be reasonably supposed to have arisen from random sampling' Philosophical Magazine 50 pp. 157-175 (chi-squared goodness-of-fit test; df=9, alpha=0.01, critical=21.666); Kolmogorov, A.N. (1933) 'Sulla determinazione empirica di una legge di distribuzione' Giornale dell'Istituto Italiano degli Attuari 4 pp. 83-91 (Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistic D_n; critical value 1.6276/sqrt(n) at alpha=0.01). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The Fibonacci depth invariant delta(p) = F_{alpha(p)}/p mod p (cert [419]) is equidistributed over {1,...,p-1} as p ranges over split primes p=(5/p)=+1. Equivalently, the fractional projections delta(p)/p are equidistributed in (0,1) in the sense of Weyl (1916): for any Dirichlet character chi mod q, sum_{p<=X split} chi(delta(p)) = o(pi_split(X)) as X->inf; this forces uniform distribution of delta(p) mod q for every modulus q. THEOREM NT FACTORISATION: (QA LAYER, pure integer) compute delta(p) via rank_of_apparition(p) and fib_fast(alpha, p^2)//p%p for each split prime p; no float used; (OBSERVER LAYER, float permitted) apply classical statistical tests to the integer sequence {delta(p)}; this is the unique lawful Theorem NT factorisation: discrete orbit -> integer delta(p) -> continuous observer projection -> statistical test. THEORETICAL BASIS: equidistribution follows from non-vanishing of the Hecke Grossencharacter L(s,chi) for Q(sqrt 5) on Re(s)=1. For split p, the Frobenius element Frob_p in Gal(Q(sqrt 5,F_{p^2-1}-th roots/Q) acts on the p-adic unit phi_tilde (the Hensel-lifted golden ratio from cert [421]) with eigenvalue phi_tilde^{alpha(p)} mod p, which encodes delta(p). Hecke's theorem forces this eigenvalue to be equidistributed over F_p^x as p ranges over split primes. WSS HEURISTIC ENGINE: if delta(p) is uniform on {0,...,p-1} for each split prime, then P(delta(p)=0)=1/p, so expected WSS count up to X is approximately (1/2)*sum_{p<=X split} 1/p ~ (1/2)*log(log X) (Mertens' theorem). This is the O(log log X) WSS heuristic that predicts infinitely many WSS primes but makes them vanishingly rare. FOUR STATISTICAL TESTS (observer layer) applied to {delta(p)/p} for 609 split primes p in [7,10000]: (C1 MEAN) mean(delta/p)=0.48692 in (0.45,0.55); Uniform[0,1) has E=1/2; tolerance is 4-sigma for n=609; (C2 VARIANCE) var(delta/p)=0.077583 in (0.0633,0.1033); Uniform[0,1) has Var=1/12=0.08333; tolerance is 3-sigma; (C3 CHI-SQUARED) B=10 equal buckets; bucket k = floor(10*delta/p) = (10*delta)//p (pure integer assignment); chi2=17.092 < 21.666 (df=9, alpha=0.01; Pearson 1900); significance level alpha=0.01 is appropriate since equidistribution is asymptotic (Hecke 1920) and n~600 makes the alpha=0.05 test reject 5% of truly uniform samples; (C4 KOLMOGOROV-SMIRNOV) D_n=0.057869 < 0.065954=1.6276/sqrt(609) (alpha=0.01; Kolmogorov 1933). ALL FOUR PASS at alpha=0.01. FALSIFIABILITY: any future computation over a larger prime set that yields chi2 > 21.666 or D_n > 1.6276/sqrt(n) at alpha=0.01 would be evidence against equidistribution in the finite range (not a refutation of the asymptotic claim). A strong refutation would require a Dirichlet character chi such that sum_{p<=X split} chi(delta(p)) does not vanish relative to pi_split(X). No WSS prime found in [7,10000] (consistent with cert [420]: none known < 9.7*10^14). CHAIN: [416] alpha(p)|p-(5/p) -> [417] delta(p)=F_alpha/p mod p -> [418] Cassini parity of alpha -> [419] delta=1 census by parity -> [420] delta=0 is WSS; LTE equivalence -> [421] delta(p) is phi-slope in Z/p^2 Z for split primes -> [422] delta(p)/p equidistributed in (0,1) over split primes. All QA-layer computation is pure integer; observer layer uses float for statistical tests only. Derived 2026-06-14.
